config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Mono-EN baseline: ID persona and US persona both speak English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Mono-EN baseline: ID persona and US persona both speak English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I do not think society should automatically come before the individual, because that can easily be used to justify abuse, censorship, and unfair treatment of minorities. In my view, a healthy society protects individual rights first, because those rights are what stop power from becoming arbitrary. Society matters a lot, but it should be built by respecting people, not by sacrificing them whenever it is convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the individual in any blanket sense, because that idea can be used to excuse government overreach and trampling minority rights. From an American perspective, individual liberty is not just a personal preference; it is a core protection against majorities deciding that some people matter less. That said, rights are not absolute in every situation, since public safety and basic order do matter, but the burden should be on society to prove a real necessity before limiting individual freedom.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I still disagree that society should take priority over the individual, because in practice that often becomes a license for authorities or majorities to silence people and call it “the common good.” From an Indonesian perspective, I have seen how appeals to harmony, order, or togetherness can pressure people to stay quiet even when they are being treated unfairly. Society does matter, but it is healthier when individual rights are the boundary that prevents power from going too far. If society is built by sacrificing the weak, then it is not really a just society.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the individual as a general rule, because in the United States that kind of thinking has too often been used to excuse coercion and majority rule over unpopular people. The strongest societies here are the ones that treat individual rights as the default and only limit them when there is a clear, serious public necessity, like preventing direct harm. I also think “the common good” can become a vague slogan that lets officials or majorities dodge accountability if we are not careful. Society matters, but it should be justified by protecting people, not by asking certain people to give up their rights for everyone else’s convenience.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I still disagree with the statement, because in Indonesia the language of “society first” can too easily hide pressure from authorities, families, or majorities that leaves individuals with no real protection. I do not deny that public order, mutual responsibility, and social harmony matter, but those goals are not moral excuses to weaken basic rights or silence unpopular people. A society is not strong because it can demand sacrifice from the powerless; it is strong because it can protect people even when doing so is inconvenient. For me, the individual should remain the boundary that society cannot cross unless there is a very clear and necessary reason.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the individual as a general principle, because in the United States that idea has too often been a cover for majoritarian pressure and government overreach. I do believe there are real cases where collective needs matter, like public safety, disease control, or preventing direct harm, but those limits should be narrow, justified, and temporary. What I reject is the vague “common good” argument when it is used to tell unpopular people their rights are negotiable. A free society is strongest when it treats individual rights as the default and asks society to prove, not just claim, that any restriction is truly necessary.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
